div.head: The Chargé in Iran (Merriam) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 48. Unprecedented flood has occurred at Mehabad (formerly Saujbulak) causing destruction of 500 houses and the loss of more than 25 lives. American lives and property there believed, safe. Also a severe earthquake has shaken Tabriz. Americans there are safe but extent of the damage if any to mission property is unknown at present. Recently there have been serious floods and earthquakes in several parts of the country. The Shah has personally contributed substantial relief funds.

Improved atmosphere now observable, would be strengthened by a direct telegram of friendly sympathy from the President to His Majesty. So far as I am aware thus far no chief of state has so acted. No allusion shall be made directly or indirectly to the American missions at the localities above mentioned.
